title: error configuring CNI network plugin: exec: "nsenter": executable file not found in $PATH
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman run -d -it centos:7

2. podman exec -it container ping www.google.com


**Describe the results you received:**

```
Error: error configuring CNI network plugin: exec: "nsenter": executable file not found in $PATH
``` 

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
3.2.3
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman-3.2.3-0.11.module_el8.x86_64
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

comments.body: I think its missing following package `util-linux` could you please try installing with `yum install util-linux`.

@flouthoc where? on the host package `util-linux` is already installed.

**Update**: `util-linux` is already installed in both host and container.
@statiksof what is the output of `which nsenter` on host.
also does error occurs after step 1 or step 2 ?

> also does error occurs after step 1 or step 2 ?

after 2

> @statiksof what is the output of `which nsenter` on host.

somehow I am having problems running `which` in the container (command not found) but on the host the output is `/usr/bin/nsenter`

@statiksof on **host** does `echo $PATH` contains path `/usr/bin/` or `/usr/bin/nsenter`. If not could you please add path to PATH variable and try again.

Also we don't need `nsenter` on container only on host.
@flouthoc PATH contains `/usr/bin`, I added manually `/usr/bin/nsenter` but didn't change anything.

`nsenter` must be in $PATH on the host and not the container.
Can you execute `nsenter` in your shell?
@statiksof What is the output of `command -v nsenter` on your host.
@Luap99 I am running in rootless mode, `command -v nsenter` for the user returns `/bin/nsenter`.

I also activated debug mode and there are other errors/warnings before `nsenter`:

```
level=debug msg="Not configuring container store" time="2021-11-01T16:08:59+01:00" level=debug msg="Initializing event backend file" time="2021-11-01T16:08:59+01:00" level=debug msg="configured OCI runtime kata initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime kata: invalid argument" time="2021-11-01T16:08:59+01:00" level=debug msg="configured OCI runtime runsc initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime runsc: invalid argument" time="2021-11-01T16:08:59+01:00" level=debug msg="configured OCI runtime crun initialization failed: no valid executable found for OCI runtime crun: invalid argument" time="2021-11-01T16:08:59+01:00" level=debug msg="Using OCI runtime \"/usr/bin/runc\"" Error: error configuring CNI network plugin: exec: "nsenter": executable file not found in $PATH) 
```

You can ignore the other warnings in the log.
Is `/bin` in your $PATH? How do you execute the `podman exec` command. It does not makes sense that this error only occurs for `podman exec`, the same code is used for `podman run`.

`/bin` is in the $PATH. After rebooting the machine I no longer can see the container, is this related?

```
podman ps -a
ERRO[0000] error joining network namespace for container ae18061d6e35368780eb384f44932ffbfce294301ae6a304fc43132beb72335b: error retrieving network namespace at /tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88: unknown FS magic on "/tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88": 58465342 
Error: error joining network namespace of container ae18061d6e35368780eb384f44932ffbfce294301ae6a304fc43132beb72335b: error retrieving network namespace at /tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88: unknown FS magic on "/tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88": 58465342
```

> `/bin` is in the $PATH. After rebooting the machine I no longer can see the container, is this related?
> 
> ```
> podman ps -a
> ERRO[0000] error joining network namespace for container ae18061d6e35368780eb384f44932ffbfce294301ae6a304fc43132beb72335b: error retrieving network namespace at /tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88: unknown FS magic on "/tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88": 58465342 
> Error: error joining network namespace of container ae18061d6e35368780eb384f44932ffbfce294301ae6a304fc43132beb72335b: error retrieving network namespace at /tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88: unknown FS magic on "/tmp/podman-run-500/netns/cni-9d9c6762-6e01-936a-1bb3-1c0b6b3dbe88": 58465342
> ```

Is your /tmp on tmpfs? The podman directories under /tmp must absolutely be removed after reboot otherwise serious bugs can happen.
You should also make sure that you have a proper logins session, e.g. do not use sudo/su.

I close this issue as the main branch no longer depends on `nsenter`.
Either way something is wrong in your environment. $PATH should be the same for podman exec and podman run,  so it does not makes sense that only exec is failing.

This issue should not be re-opened if the new version doesn't depend on `nsenter` but for anyone else finding this when searching for a solution like I did: I ran into this problem when trying to run `podman pod create` as a rootless user. it worked when I ran it from my own shell, but for whatever reason when it was run within a script as the exact same user I ran into this same error. Didn't have any issues with any other podman commands, except for `podman network create` which I had not previously been using.

I managed to fix it by prepending the `$PATH` environment variable like so:

```bash
PATH=$PATH podman pod create
```

I have no idea why the `$PATH` wasn't visible within this specific situation (rootless and scripted) but the fix works, so I'm using this until I can upgrade versions.
